dataset.development_iteration: The v1 results were inspected before this profile and its gates were frozen. The task prompts and evidence labels are unchanged.

arms.name: thin-leantoken-context-bundle
arms.candidate: one context bundle plus at most one focused search; no read, outline, or files calls

tasks.prompt: Fix IPv6 address handling in Flask. session_transaction loses the IPv6 host when saving cookies, and Flask.run parses SERVER_NAME=[::1]:8080 incorrectly. Add regression coverage for both behaviors without changing IPv4 host:port behavior.

Fix render.AsciiJSON so non-BMP Unicode such as emoji round-trips through JSON. JSON unicode escapes must use a UTF-16 surrogate pair instead of a five-digit escape. Preserve current BMP and ASCII behavior and add a regression test.

Fix app.render and res.render for a view name ending in a dot. app.render('index.', callback) must report the normal failed-to-lookup-view error through the callback instead of throwing because require receives an empty module name. Add regression coverage.

Fix tokio::fs::File so a second poll_write does not panic after spawn_mandatory_blocking failed on the previous write. The failed spawn currently leaves State::Idle without its buffer. Cover poll_write and poll_write_vectored and add a regression test for a shut-down blocking pool.

interpretation: Development iteration after inspecting v1. The same four tasks and labels are rerun with fresh randomized native controls. The candidate is intentionally turn-bounded because v1 showed that provider turns, not source bytes, dominated total input.
